mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or flat during tightening periods when INDPRO declines, or the signal's predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher consumer price index levels erode real corporate margins and prompt anticipatory rate hikes, creating immediate downward pressure on S&P 500 prices over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC is consistently negative or near zero over rolling 2-year windows, or CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% annualized signals a demand contraction severe enough to trigger earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive quarterly returns in more than 50% of quarters following sub-1.0% GDP growth, or the threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates healthy labor absorption, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k while UNRATE change is <= 0.1pp
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply chains, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following sharp INDPRO drops during recovery phases, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
